vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is severely depleted. P1 (Yellow Pot) is devoid of visible foliage. P3 (Black Pot) contains the white rabbit anchor but lacks the primary Pothos specimen. P2/P4 (Black Pot) shows a single, isolated, etiolated sprout.

inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss (Pothos missing, only anchor remains)
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (Only a single sprout remains)

plant_audit.P1: No biological material detected. Soil appears undisturbed.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present; host plant (Pothos) is absent.
plant_audit.P4: Single green sprout visible; appears to be a remnant or incidental growth rather than the registered Mexican Mint/Silver Guest.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears consistently dark and moist across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: White mineral/perlite-like fragments present in P3 and P4.

temporal_deltas.EARLIEST_to_T-1: Total loss of visible plant structure; transition to near-total dormancy or death.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Static state; no recovery or new growth observed.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical failure. The lack of foliage suggests either total senescence or recent removal of specimens. The single sprout in P4 is the only sign of life, though it shows signs of etiolation (stretching due to lack of sufficient light).
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of a white rabbit anchor in an otherwise empty pot (P3) suggests a decorative setup that has lost its botanical component.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment has undergone a catastrophic decline. The registry indicates a diverse collection, but the visual evidence confirms that the pots are essentially empty, save for one solitary, struggling sprout. The environment is currently non-viable for the registered species.
